Function to convert a base 10 string to base b:

string to_base_b(string s, int b) {
    if (s == "0") return "0";
    string res;
    while (s != "0") {
        int rem = s.back() - '0';
        res += (rem % b) + '0';
        s = subtract_one(s);
        s = divide_by_b(s, b);
    }
    reverse(res.begin(), res.end());
    return res;
}

int mod_string(const string &s, int b) {
    int res = 0;
    for (char c : s) {
        res = (res * 10 + (c - '0')) % b;
    }
    return res;
}

So, the code outline is:

class Solution {
public:
    int countNumbers(string l, string r, int b) {
        // Convert l and r to base b.
        string l_b = to_base_b(l, b);
        string r_b = to_base_b(r, b);
        
        // Compute f(r_b) and f(l_b).
        int f_r = f(r_b, b);
        int f_l = f(l_b, b);
        
        // Check if l_b is valid.
        bool valid = is_non_decreasing(l_b);
        
        int ans = (f_r - f_l + (valid ? 1 : 0)) % MOD;
        if (ans < 0) ans += MOD; // Ensure non-negative.
        return ans;
    }
    
private:
    // Helper functions: to_base_b, f, is_non_decreasing.
};

}

So, the function to compute the remainder of a base 10 string modulo b is:

int mod_string(const string &s, int b) {
    int res = 0;
    for (char c : s) {
        res = (res * 10 + (c - '0')) % b;
    }
    return res;
}

So, the code outline is:

class Solution {
public:
    int countNumbers(string l, string r, int b) {
        // Convert l and r to base b.
        string l_b = to_base_b(l, b);
        string r_b = to_base_b(r, b);
        
        // Compute f(r_b) and f(l_b).
        int f_r = f(r_b, b);
        int f_l = f(l_b, b);
        
        // Check if l_b is valid.
        bool valid = is_non_decreasing(l_b);
        
        int ans = (f_r - f_l + (valid ? 1 : 0)) % MOD;
        if (ans < 0) ans += MOD; // Ensure non-negative.
        return ans;
    }
    
private:
    // Helper functions: to_base_b, f, is_non_decreasing.
};
